notes: A single transcript event is unusually large: kiro-cli writes an append-only JSONL transcript under ~/.kiro/sessions/cli/<uuid>.jsonl, one \n-terminated record per event, and a ToolResults event embeds the FULL tool-output JSON on one line — so a single tool call that reads a sizable file (or a long single-sentence reply) yields ONE oversized JSONL line. kiro is a line_based adapter (core/adapters/inbound/agents/kirocli/agent.go: FilesUnderRoot{JSONLineParser}), so the line runs through irrlicht's SHARED tailer (core/pkg/tailer/tailer.go: bufio.NewReaderSize + readLineCapped, maxTranscriptLineSize=64MB, issue #270): a line under the cap parses normally, a line above it is logged-and-skipped past — neither stalls. Recorded with the INTERACTIVE kiro-cli driver (tmux TUI: send + wait_turn keeps the session alive so the daemon observes the working→ready settle; the TUI is the only mode that persists a session file, FINDINGS.md §7). SESSION-IDENTITY: the live TUI emits a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready at launch; session_birth asserts only 'a session appears in ready' UNPINNED so it can bind that pre-session; turn_start carries no session pin so the matcher binds the FIRST working — necessarily the UUID-keyed session, since the pre-session never goes working. ROBUSTNESS CLAIM: the 'oversized line' size property is NOT a phase-DSL form; the absence of a parser stall is INFERRED from this clean arc completing (no stuck working, no waiting). The genuine >64 MB cap behavior is covered by the agent-agnostic core/pkg/tailer/large_line_test.go (readLineCapped / skip-past, #270), not by this live recording — a live CLI cannot be coerced into a multi-megabyte single line, so this recording is a sanity-check that the arc survives a large single line. OBSERVATIONS: kiro JSONL carries no model/token/cost (those live in the unread <uuid>.json sidecar), so the recording's observation vector is model=unknown, cost=0, tokens=0; this spec asserts NO hard model/cost/tokens metric (cost_nonzero/tokens_nonzero false, model unpinned) and relies on the state arc only.

text: Session appears in ready within ~2s of TUI launch (binds the pre-session UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> row can't cascade failures)
Large-tool-result / long-response prompt drives ready → working (binds the first working = the UUID-keyed session)

Oversized JSONL line is processed (or skipped past, under the 64 MB cap); the text-only AssistantMessage lands as turn_done; working → ready. The completion of this transition is the spec's parser-resilience proof — had the tailer wedged on the long line it would never fire.
